examples.title: Basic Command Line Arguments Program
examples.description: Demonstrates basic usage of argc and argv to add two numbers
examples.explanation: This program accepts two numbers as command line arguments, validates that exactly 2 arguments are provided (excluding program name), converts them from strings to integers using atoi(), adds them, and prints the result.

examples.title: File Processing with Command Line Arguments
examples.description: Opens and displays contents of a file specified via command line
examples.explanation: This program takes a filename as command line argument, attempts to open it, handles file open errors, reads the entire file character by character using fgetc(), displays contents, and properly closes the file.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is file size

examples.title: Using strtol for Robust Error Handling
examples.description: Demonstrates proper error handling while converting strings to integers
examples.explanation: strtol() provides better error handling than atoi(). It sets errno on overflow, endptr points to first non-digit character allowing detection of partial conversions, and handles larger numbers (long vs int).
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is string length

examples.title: Using getopt for Option Parsing
examples.description: Demonstrates parsing command-line options using getopt() function
examples.explanation: getopt() parses Unix-style command line options. The option string "hvf:" defines expected options where ':' indicates an option requires an argument. optarg automatically points to the argument value. optind tracks non-option arguments.
